summaryTemplate: {{className}} is the {{patternRole}}. It declares one visit() per element type so new operations can be added across a hierarchy without editing the element classes. Pattern body lives at {{file}}:{{lineStart}}-{{lineEnd}}.
chosenRationaleTemplate: Tagged as Visitor because {{className}} declares virtual visit/accept methods (lines {{lineStart}}-{{lineEnd}}) — the double-dispatch shape that externalises operations from the element hierarchy.

visitor_visit_method.note: A visit operation. One per element type; adding a new operation means adding a new visitor, not editing every element.

runnerUpComparisonTemplates.Strategy: Strategy varies one algorithm behind an interface. Visitor adds a family of operations over many element types via accept/visit double dispatch — the goal is extending operations, not swapping one.
